missed_facts: Under the Finnish Tax Administration's decision on the calculation bases for withholding tax percentages on wage income for 2026 (Verohallinnon päätös ennakonpidätysprosenttien laskentaperusteista palkkatuloa varten ...
vuodelle 2026), the withholding tax percentage (ennakonpidätysprosentti) is confirmed in increments of half a percentage point and rounded upwards.
It can be at most 60.0 percent.

missed_facts: According to the Finnish Tax Administration's 2026 withholding decision (section 4.3.1), the employee pension insurance contribution (eläkevakuutusmaksu / TyEL employee share) used as a deduction from withholding-taxable wages and seafarer income is 7.30 percent of the gross pay.

The contribution starts to accrue from the beginning of the calendar month following the month in which the employee turns 17, and continues until the upper age limit of the insurance obligation is reached.
That upper age limit is staggered by birth cohort: 68 years for persons born in 1957 or earlier, 69 years for persons born 1958-1961, and 70 years for persons born in 1962 or later.

matched_facts: The actual restaurant/catering service is supplied by the restaurant directly to the employee, not to the employer.
missed_facts: KHO held that 'arvosetelin hankkimista ja luovutusta ei ollut pidettava hyodykkeen ostamisena ja myymisena' — the acquisition and transfer of the voucher is NOT a supply of goods/services for VAT purposes.

matched_facts: (a) Matti's own 100,000 EUR share: TAX-FREE under TVL 85 sec.
(b) The other four colleagues' 100,000 EUR shares each: TAXABLE as 'muuta veronalaista ansiotuloa' (other taxable earned income).
missed_facts: His own 100,000 EUR share is verovapaa arpajaisvoitto.
Note: the entire 500,000 EUR is, in the first instance, his tax-free lottery winning under TVL 85 sec.
Standard indirect-cost ratio: 22% of direct costs.
VAT are 8.80–14.00 EUR; outside that band, the value equals the direct costs incl.
- Hospital/school/daycare/similar institution staff (laitosruokailu): 6.60 EUR/meal.

missed_facts: Persons born in 1957 or earlier: insurance obligation ends at the end of the calendar month in which the worker turns 68.
Persons born 1958–1961: insurance obligation ends at the end of the calendar month in which the worker turns 69.

(a) Withholding rate on the payment to the friend (the substitute): If the substitute has not presented a tax card (verokorttia), the primary courier — acting as the payer to the substitute — must withhold tax at 60 %.
In both cases, the substitute's missing tax card triggers the 60 % default rate.

(a) Annual amounts: - Bicycle commuter (12 km each way × 2 × 220 days = 5,280 km/year):   Bicycles, including e-bikes (sähköpyörä), are deducted as a flat 100 euro per year — distance and number of commuting days do not matter.
So the gross matkakuluvähennys is 100 euro/year before omavastuu.
After the standard omavastuu, in practice this means no net deduction for most taxpayers, since the omavastuu exceeds 100 euro.
- Moped commuter (same 5,280 km/year):   Moped (mopo) is deducted at 0,13 e/km.
So 5,280 km × 0,13 e = 686.40 euro/year (gross, before omavastuu).
The maximum credit is 3 430 euro.
(b) Increase for dependent children — and for being 65+: - Each alaikäinen lapsi (under-18 child) of whom the taxpayer is huoltaja at year-end increases the maximum by 105 euro.
Single huoltaja: the increase is doubled (210 euro) for each child of whom they are sole huoltaja.
So the child-based increase is 2 × 105 = 210 euro (or 2 × 210 = 420 euro if the taxpayer is a single huoltaja and is the sole huoltaja of those two children).

missed_facts: (c) Finnish tax on the 14,000 EUR rental income alone:  Assuming the 14,000 EUR is the NET pääomatulo after deductible vuokraustoiminnan menot (e.g.
yhtiövastike, repairs, depreciation), and Liisa has no other capital income in Finland for the year:  - The whole 14,000 EUR is below the 30,000 EUR threshold, so the entire amount is taxed at 30 %.
Her Finnish tax liability on the rental income alone is thus 4,200 euros.

matched_facts: So Tommi must apply a 60% withholding rate to Juhani's pay.
missed_facts: In both cases — toimeksianto (work-trade) and työsuhde (employment) — if the recipient (Juhani) does not present a tax card, the payer must withhold 60% ("Jos sijainen ei ole esittänyt verokorttia, ruokalähetin on toimitettava ennakonpidätys 60 prosentin suuruisena.").
